title.value: OTC: Optimal Tool Calls via Reinforcement Learning
title.evidence: Tool-integrated reasoning (TIR) augments large language models (LLMs) with the ability to invoke external tools...

authors.evidence: Hongru Wangα, Cheng Qianβ, Wanjun Zhongδ, Xiusi Chenβ, Jiahao Qiuσ, Shijue Huangµ, Bowen Jinβ, Mengdi Wangσ, Kam-Fai Wongα, Heng Jiβ...
authors.notes: Authors are affiliated with multiple institutions.

value: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
affiliations.evidence: αThe Chinese University of Hong Kong, βUniversity of Illinois Urbana-Champaign σPrinceton University, $^ \delta \mathsf { S u n }$ Yat-sen University, $\mu$ Hong Kong University of Science and Technology...

current_issues.value: Existing reinforcement learning methods often overlook the efficiency and cost associated with tool usage, leading to excessive tool calls or insufficient tool use, which compromises answer quality.
current_issues.evidence: While reinforcement learning (RL) has shown promise in improving TIR by optimizing final answer correctness, existing approaches often overlook the efficiency and cost associated with tool usage...

target.value: Develop a method that balances answer correctness with efficient tool usage, optimizing the number of tool calls needed for correct answers.
target.evidence: We aim to develop such a method based on a foundational assumption: for each question and each model, there exists an optimal number of tool calls...

framework_innovation.value: OTC-PO is the first RL-based framework that explicitly optimizes tool-use efficiency in tool-integrated reasoning.
framework_innovation.evidence: To the best of our knowledge, this is the first RL-based framework that explicitly optimizes tool-use efficiency in TIR...

metric_innovation.value: Introduces a new metric called tool productivity (TP) that measures the effectiveness and efficiency of tool calls during inference.
metric_innovation.evidence: We additionally define a new metric, tool productivity (TP), which measures the effectiveness and efficiency of tool calls during inference...

algorithm_innovation.value: Proposes OTC-PPO and OTC-GRPO, two variants of the OTC-PO framework that integrate with existing RL algorithms.
algorithm_innovation.evidence: We instantiate OTC-PO in two variants: OTC-PPO and OTC-GRPO, tailored to different underlying RL algorithms...

provenance.section_header: 3.3 OTC-PO: Optimal Tool Call-controlled Policy Optimization

performance_improvement.value: OTC-PO reduces tool calls by up to 73.1% and improves tool productivity by up to 229.4% while maintaining comparable answer accuracy.
performance_improvement.evidence: Experiments with Qwen-2.5 and Qwen-Math across multiple QA benchmarks show that our approach reduces tool calls by up to 73.1% and improves tool productivity by up to 229.4%...

tool_call_reduction.value: The OTC-PO framework significantly reduces the number of tool calls required to achieve correct answers.
tool_call_reduction.evidence: OTC-PPO achieves significant improvement in terms of TC and TP compared with Search-R1...

model_size_effect.value: Larger models tend to over-rely on external tool calls unless explicitly penalized for such behavior.
model_size_effect.evidence: If we look at the Search-R1, we can find that 3B model tends to use less tool calls compared with 7B model...

generalization_performance.value: OTC-PO demonstrates better generalization capabilities across various benchmarks and out-of-domain evaluations.
generalization_performance.evidence: OTC-PPO outperforms the Search-R1-PPO in terms of EM on all benchmarks with Qwen2.5-7B-Base model with much less tool calls...

case_study_insights.value: OTC-PO effectively balances internal reasoning and tool usage, leading to fewer tool calls while maintaining answer accuracy.
case_study_insights.evidence: Our proposed method not only minimizes external tool calls, but also maximizes internal reasoning capabilities of LLMs...

task_definition.value: The task involves generating a correct answer with the minimal number of tool calls, defined as the cost of tool usage.
task_definition.evidence: The objective of the task is to generate the correct answer $\hat { a }$ with the minimal cost of tools in the full trajectory...

base_reward.value: The base reward is assigned based on answer correctness, where correct answers receive a reward of 1 and incorrect ones receive 0.
base_reward.evidence: Most prior predominantly focuses on rule-based correctness rewards...

efficiency_reward.value: The efficiency reward is designed to penalize excessive tool calls while rewarding optimal tool usage.
efficiency_reward.evidence: Instead of only considering the correctness (and optionally format) of the answer, we argue it is important to consider the effectiveness and efficiency...

provenance.section_header: 3.3 OTC-PO: Optimal Tool Call-controlled Policy Optimization

final_reward.value: The final reward integrates both correctness and efficiency, ensuring that tool usage is minimized while maintaining accuracy.
final_reward.evidence: We regard the tool-integrated reward $r_{tool}$ as a coefficient of conventional reward function...

provenance.section_header: 3.3 OTC-PO: Optimal Tool Call-controlled Policy Optimization

algorithm_integration.value: OTC-PO integrates with existing RL algorithms like PPO and GRPO to optimize tool usage during training.
algorithm_integration.evidence: We implement OTC-PPO and OTC-GRPO as two typical methods without losing the adaptability and generalization...

provenance.section_header: 3.3 OTC-PO: Optimal Tool Call-controlled Policy Optimization

datasets.value: The datasets used include NQ and HotpotQA for search tasks, and a dataset from ToRL for code tasks.
datasets.evidence: We mainly follow the Search-R1 [13] and ToRL [14] setting and use same baselines and datasets for the fair and comprehensive evaluation...

models.value: The models evaluated include Qwen-2.5-3B/7B-Base and Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B/7B-Base.
models.evidence: Using multiple LLMs—including Qwen-2.5-3B/7B-Base and Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B/7B-Base...

parameters.value: Parameters include adjustments for maximum tool calls and reward scaling factors.
parameters.evidence: We set the max turn (maximal number of tool calls) in Search-R1 (ToRL) to 4 (3) to better study the effects of our methods...

resources.value: Experiments were conducted on 8 A100-80G GPUs.
resources.evidence: We conduct our experiments on 8 A100-80G GPU and re-produce the results of Search-R1 and ToRL independently...

search_tool_results.value: OTC-PPO achieved an EM score of 0.355 with a tool call reduction of 41.9% compared to Search-R1.
search_tool_results.evidence: OTC-PPO achieved significant improvement in terms of TC and TP compared with Search-R1...

code_tool_results.value: OTC-GRPO showed promising preliminary results in code tasks with improved tool utilization.
code_tool_results.evidence: We already observe similar findings as mentioned above and will release more results soon...
code_tool_results.notes: Results are preliminary and further analysis is needed.

behavior_analysis.value: Analysis shows OTC-PO reduces unnecessary tool calls while maintaining or improving accuracy.
behavior_analysis.evidence: Our method achieves same answers with baseline using fewer tool calls in mostly 80% of test cases...
